invalid_review_attempts.reason: Review run failed with an API 429 rate-limit error before producing structured output (auth_or_cli_error / structured_output_missing in the normalized receipt); no review content was produced or accepted. Preserved and retried.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-332 is a descriptor-only RP10.P06 permission-binding slice (M05 permission/audit binding tail + M06 synthetic fixture head) over 10 planned units. The pack-level claim is correctly scoped: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, with production_ready_flag=intake_core_permission_binding_slice_descriptor_verified (descriptor verification only, not a runtime/enterprise-trust claim). Verified against plan, baseline, manifest, and contract: 10 RP10 units RP10.P06.M05.S16-RP10.P06.M06.S03, deliverable split security_audit:4/test:4/implementation:2, micro split M05:7/M06:3 — no drift; CP00-333 continues at RP10.P06.M06.S04. Descriptor-only boundaries hold: all rows descriptor_only:true/runtime_execution:false; a pattern scan of packages/intake/src/*.js found no write/exec/network/DB calls; the nine per-row leak guards in validators.js all resolve to real inherited row properties (CP330->CP331->CP332 ROW_EXTRAS chain), so validation is non-vacuous. Authority boundary is intact: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false; Hermes is used only for the H10 descriptor evidence packet/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape ..._no_write) and is not embedded in product runtime. No real client data is loaded (loads_real_fixture_data=false, synthetic_only=true). Tests in model.test.js are descriptor assertions with no runtime dispatch. Review-hardening integrity is sound: prior attempt-01 (API 429) is correctly recorded as invalid_not_accepted with parsed_review=null and zero valid review receipts; no premature/forged claude-review-result.json exists. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are absent as expected for this pending-review stage of the hardening sequence and are not a defect. One minor (P3, non-blocking) observation: the audit_event_expectation security_audit row lacks a dedicated validator guard, though its audit-write boundary is still attested at the descriptor and no_write_attestation levels. Claude remains a read-only reviewer, not final approval authority; human approval outside this receipt governs any enterprise-trust claim. No P0/P1/P2 issues; this review does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: audit_event_expectation row has no dedicated validator leak-guard
findings.details: validateIntakeCoreCp332PermissionBindingSliceDescriptor asserts row-specific properties for 9 of the 10 row titles (security_trimming_proof, permission_fixture, allowed_test, denied_test, cross_tenant_test, leak_prevention_test, permission_matrix_row, view_decision_binding, search_decision_binding) but has no dedicated guard for the M05 'Audit event expectation' (audit_event_expectation) security_audit row. The row does carry writes_audit_event:false and audit_event_body_included:false via the inherited CP330 ROW_EXTRAS (service.js:1485-1488), and the audit-write boundary is additionally attested at the descriptor/case-set aggregate (freezeCp332Result writes_audit_event:false, service.js:1736) and in the no_write_attestation (writes_audit_event:false). Only the generic descriptor_only/runtime_execution/customer_safe/raw_payload assertions apply to this specific row, so a future regression that altered its audit-specific extras would not be caught by the per-row validator the way the other nine titles are.
findings.recommendation: In a future hardening pass, add a dedicated guard asserting rows.audit_event_expectation.writes_audit_event === false and audit_event_body_included === false, mirroring the other per-row leak guards, so the audit-event security row has the same regression protection as its peers.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Boundary is already attested at the descriptor and no_write_attestation levels and validation currently passes; does not gate CP00-332 closeout. Recommended as a follow-up validator-coverage hardening item.
